tweets.title: REPORT: Ukraine War Casualties Surpass 2 Million A new study by the Center for Strategic and International Studies estimates that total casualties in the Russia–Ukraine war have exceeded 2 million since 2022. Findings: 🇷🇺 Russia has suffered ~1.4 million casualties, including an estimated 450,000 killed. Russia is reportedly losing 30,000–34,000 troops per month, exceeding its estimated monthly recruitment of 27,000. 🇺🇦 Ukraine has recently gained more territory than it has lost, while expanding long-range strikes into Russia. Ukrainian forces have suffered between 525,000 and 625,000 total casualties, including 125,000 to 150,000 fatalities since February 2022. Ukraine maintains roughly 250,000 troops on the active front line, directly facing an estimated 400,000 Russian troops. The figures are estimates from CSIS and have not been independently verified by the governments involved.
tweets.content:
REPORT: Ukraine War Casualties Surpass 2 Million

A new study by the Center for Strategic and International Studies estimates that total casualties in the Russia–Ukraine war have exceeded 2 million since 2022.

Findings:

🇷🇺 Russia has suffered ~1.4 million casualties, including an estimated 450,000 killed. Russia is reportedly losing 30,000–34,000 troops per month, exceeding its estimated monthly recruitment of 27,000.

🇺🇦 Ukraine has recently gained more territory than it has lost, while expanding long-range strikes into Russia. Ukrainian forces have suffered between 525,000 and 625,000 total casualties, including 125,000 to 150,000 fatalities since February 2022. Ukraine maintains roughly 250,000 troops on the active front line, directly facing an estimated 400,000 Russian troops.

The figures are estimates from CSIS and have not been independently verified by the governments involved.

tweets.title: Indian Army Begins Rollout of Integrated Battle Groups (IBGs) The Indian Army has begun implementing the Integrated Battle Group (IBG) concept, starting with the China-focused 17 Mountain Strike Corps. - IBGs are self-sufficient, agile combat formations of around 5,000 personnel, designed to launch offensive operations in under 24 hours. - The 23 Division will be the first to be reorganised into two IBGs, with the rollout expected to continue through mid-2027. - Each IBG will integrate infantry, armour, artillery, engineers, and signals under a single command. Originally envisioned by Gen Bipin Rawat, the IBG concept is a key part of the Army's transformation towards faster, more flexible, and theatre-specific operations along India's borders. @adgpi @easterncomd @NorthernComd_IA @westerncomd_IA @HQ_IDS_India @SpokespersonMoD @DefenceMinIndia
tweets.content:
Indian Army Begins Rollout of Integrated Battle Groups (IBGs)

The Indian Army has begun implementing the Integrated Battle Group (IBG) concept, starting with the China-focused 17 Mountain Strike Corps.

- IBGs are self-sufficient, agile combat formations of around 5,000 personnel, designed to launch offensive operations in under 24 hours.

- The 23 Division will be the first to be reorganised into two IBGs, with the rollout expected to continue through mid-2027.

- Each IBG will integrate infantry, armour, artillery, engineers, and signals under a single command.

Originally envisioned by Gen Bipin Rawat, the IBG concept is a key part of the Army's transformation towards faster, more flexible, and theatre-specific operations along India's borders.

Key discussion points included: - Freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz - Release of $6 billion in frozen Iranian funds - Establishing a direct communication channel to manage future disputes Notably, Iran's nuclear programme was not discussed, with both sides indicating it will be addressed in a future round. tweets.content:
UPDATE: US–Iran Talks in Doha End Without Major Breakthrough

The latest round of indirect U.S.–Iran talks in Doha concluded with no major breakthrough, but both sides agreed to continue negotiations.

Key discussion points included:

- Freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz
- Release of $6 billion in frozen Iranian funds
- Establishing a direct communication channel to manage future disputes

Notably, Iran's nuclear programme was not discussed, with both sides indicating it will be addressed in a future round.

Qatar described the talks as making "positive progress", while U.S. President Donald Trump said discussions on limiting Iran's nuclear programme were "moving along well."

The next round of talks is expected after the funeral ceremonies for former Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

tweets.title: BREAKING: HM Amit Shah Urges Early Report from Demographic Change Panel Union Home Minister Amit Shah has asked the High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes to submit its recommendations at the earliest. The committee, chaired by Justice (Retd.) Prakash Prabhakar Naolekar, has begun consultations with states, Union Territories, and Central ministries to examine demographic changes linked to illegal immigration and other abnormal factors. Its mandate includes: 🔹 Studying demographic shifts across India, especially in border districts, metro cities, industrial towns, and tribal regions. 🔹 Assessing the impact on national security, governance, and social cohesion. 🔹 Recommending policy, legal, and administrative measures, including mechanisms for the identification, detention, and deportation of illegal immigrants. The committee was constituted by the Ministry of Home Affairs following Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Independence Day 2025 announcement and is expected to submit its final report within one year.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: HM Amit Shah Urges Early Report from Demographic Change Panel

Union Home Minister Amit Shah has asked the High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes to submit its recommendations at the earliest.

The committee, chaired by Justice (Retd.) Prakash Prabhakar Naolekar, has begun consultations with states, Union Territories, and Central ministries to examine demographic changes linked to illegal immigration and other abnormal factors.

Its mandate includes:
🔹 Studying demographic shifts across India, especially in border districts, metro cities, industrial towns, and tribal regions.
🔹 Assessing the impact on national security, governance, and social cohesion.
🔹 Recommending policy, legal, and administrative measures, including mechanisms for the identification, detention, and deportation of illegal immigrants.

The committee was constituted by the Ministry of Home Affairs following Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Independence Day 2025 announcement and is expected to submit its final report within one year.

tweets.title: Defence Research & Development Laboratory (DRDL), Hyderabad, is planning to establish a state-of-the-art blow-down type Trisonic Wind Tunnel (TWT) with a 1.6 m × 1.6 m test section, capable of operating across a Mach number range of 0.2 to 4.5. The facility will be developed at a designated site in Hyderabad, India, to provide controlled, uniform, and steady airflow conditions for aerodynamic testing. It will support the evaluation of scaled models of rockets, tactical missiles, aircraft, launch vehicles, and other aerospace systems, enabling the generation of critical aerodynamic data across subsonic, transonic, and supersonic flight regimes.

tweets.content:
BREAKING: Pakistan Opposition Leaders Blocked from Entering Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK)

The Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JKJAAC) has accused Pakistani authorities of suppressing democratic rights after a delegation of opposition leaders was reportedly stopped from entering Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir (PoJK).

According to JKJAAC, the delegation, led by Mahmood Khan Achakzai and including former Prime Minister Shahid Khaqan Abbasi and other opposition figures, was halted at Kotli and prevented from proceeding further.

The committee also alleged restrictions on movement and supplies, describing them as part of a broader crackdown on dissent. JKJAAC reiterated that its movement is peaceful and vowed to continue protests until its demands are addressed.
